items.title: S&P 500 closes lower, Nasdaq falls more than 1% as chip stocks suffer
items.content: S&P 500 fell 1.01% to 7,457.69, Nasdaq dropped 1.4% to 25,520.24, Dow lost 406.55 pts (0.77%) to 52,146.42. Semiconductor stocks dragged markets lower after Moonshot AI unveiled new model narrowing gap with US offerings.

items.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected
items.content: CPI fell 0.4% month-over-month — biggest decline since April 2020. Core CPI flat on month, 2.6% annual rate vs 2.9% expected. Energy index slumped 5.7% in June. Fed rate hike odds in September dropped to 63% from >75%.

items.title: US stock market index (US500) at 7,458 points, down 1.01%
items.content: US500 fell to 7,458 points on July 17. Over past month index declined 0.57%, though remains 18.44% higher than a year ago.

items.title: Hong Kong Hang Seng up 1.29%, CSI 300 falls 1.85%
items.content: Hang Seng rose 1.29% led by real estate (+2.17%) and consumer cyclicals (+2.07%). Mainland China CSI 300 closed 1.85% lower at 4,698.43 amid semiconductor profit-taking.

items.title: Hang Seng climbs 0.52% to 24,340.73 as China trade beat lifts sentiment
items.content: HSI rose 127 points to 24,340.73, third gain in four sessions. China trade data beat expectations, boosting market sentiment despite Gulf geopolitical risks.

items.title: Hang Seng above 24,680 after four-session rally
items.content: Hong Kong market tracked higher four straight sessions, advancing >640 points (2.6%). Hang Seng Index above 24,680-point plateau.

items.title: Moonshot AI unveils model narrowing gap with US — triggers semiconductor selloff
items.content: Chinese startup Moonshot AI released new multimodal model with 1M-token context window. Z.ai (formerly Zhipu AI) advanced GLM-5.2 open-weight model. Nasdaq 100 futures fell as much as 2.3%.

items.title: China's AI breakthrough triggers semiconductor selloff
items.content: Chinese AI stocks remain relatively inexpensive vs US AI stocks. Housing starts at 1.427M beat 1.328M consensus. Money flows in Mag 7 neutral. Bitcoin selling alongside tech stocks.

items.title: China stocks close down on chip profit-taking
items.content: Chinese stocks ended slightly lower as investors locked in semiconductor gains and shifted capital.

items.title: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis - renewed tensions
items.content: On June 19 Trump announced renewed Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire. Iran said it closed the strait again. On June 27 JMIC widened route through Strait of Hormuz. Tensions remain elevated.

items.title: Opinion: This Is a Forever War in the Making
items.content: US views corridor as insurance against another Iranian closure of Strait of Hormuz. Ongoing military engagement raises concerns about prolonged conflict.

items.title: US core inflation falls to 2.6% in June, below expectations
items.content: Core CPI fell from 2.9% (7-month high) to 2.6%, below 2.8% expected. Shelter costs moderated (3.3% vs 3.4%). First slowdown in five months.
